metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the conditional 'if the low end is used' hedge from AC1 and state `value = -1 Do not land` as a required line, consistent with the `-1..+1` range in the ACL spec.
findings.finding: AC1 uses 'if the low end is used' to make the `value = -1 Do not land` line conditional, but the ACL range is explicitly `-1..+1`, making the -1 value non-optional. An executor could omit the -1 value line and still satisfy the literal AC wording while producing a misconfigured label.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC1 parenthetical to: 'and explicit value lines: `value = -1 Do not land`, `value = 0 No score`, `value = +1 Land this change`' (unconditional).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `gerrit create-group` + membership-convergence block for `Autosubmit Voters` (with `autolander-bot` as initial member) to `setup-project.sh`, mirroring the existing pattern for `feature-branch-drivers` and `Contributors`, in addition to the `want=` dict entry already specified in AC #3.
findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `Autosubmit Voters` to `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` dict (AC #3), but the existing provisioning pattern requires a separate `gerrit create-group` block for any group referenced in `project.config` — without it, the `want=` lookup returns nothing and Gerrit rejects the `refs/meta/config` push with an 'unknown group' error. The plan names only the `want=` dict as the edit target and omits the `create-group` stanza as a required edit.
findings.location: AC #3 / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh lines 60–107
findings.suggested_fix: Add a block immediately before the `want=` dict entry (around line 108) following the same pattern as lines 91–107: `AS_GROUP="Autosubmit Voters"; if ! $GERRIT_SSH gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF -- "$AS_GROUP"; then $GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group "$AS_GROUP" --description "Autosubmit label voters: autolander-bot only" --owner Administrators; fi; $GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AS_GROUP" --add autolander-bot >/dev/null 2>&1 || true`. Then include `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` in the `want=` dict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `setup-project.sh` must gain a full group-creation-and-membership-convergence block for `Autosubmit Voters` (create-if-absent + add `autolander-bot` as member), mirroring the existing `feature-branch-drivers` and `Contributors` patterns, in addition to the `want=` UUID dict entry.
findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `Autosubmit Voters` to the `want=` UUID dict in setup-project.sh (so the group UUID is resolved for the refs/meta/config push), but omits specifying that a corresponding group-creation-and-membership-convergence block must also be added. Without it, if `Autosubmit Voters` doesn't yet exist on the server, `gerrit ls-groups --verbose` returns no line for it, the UUID is empty, and Gerrit rejects the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error — the same failure mode the existing script comments explicitly guard against for feature-branch-drivers and Contributors.
findings.location: What section — 'Register the group's UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict'
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a block to setup-project.sh immediately before the `want=` declaration:
```bash
AV_GROUP="Autosubmit Voters"
if $GERRIT_SSH gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF -- "$AV_GROUP"; then
  echo "setup-project: group '$AV_GROUP' already exists" >&2
else
  echo "setup-project: creating group '$AV_GROUP'" >&2
  $GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group "$AV_GROUP" \
    --description "Accounts authorised to cast Autosubmit votes (autolander-bot only)"
fi
$GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AV_GROUP" --add autolander-bot >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
```
Then add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to the `want=` dict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the `value = -1 Do not land` line unconditional in AC #1 (drop the 'if the low end is used' qualifier), since the -1..+1 range already makes -1 always reachable.
findings.finding: The AC #1 value vocabulary for the -1 slot is conditional ('if the low end is used'), but the label range is already fixed at -1..+1 in both the label definition and the ACL grant — meaning the -1 value IS always reachable by Autosubmit Voters. The enum string for this reachable value is therefore never definitively defined, leaving the executor to choose a display string for a voting option that the system will accept.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #1: 'value = -1 Do not land (if the low end is used)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'value = -1 Do not land (if the low end is used)' with 'value = -1 Do not land' — the range is fixed and the value is always present.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `gerrit create-group "Autosubmit Voters"` block (with description and owner) to the plan's AC #3 / setup-project.sh spec, mirroring the feature-branch-drivers and Contributors patterns, so the group exists before the want= UUID resolution step.
findings.finding: The plan specifies adding 'Autosubmit Voters' to setup-project.sh's `want=` group-UUID dict (AC #3), but tool-verified inspection of setup-project.sh shows that the `want=` dict ONLY resolves UUIDs of groups that ALREADY EXIST on the server — it does not create them. The existing pattern (feature-branch-drivers, Contributors) requires an explicit `gerrit create-group` block BEFORE the `want=` step, or Gerrit will reject the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error. The plan omits this prerequisite creation protocol entirely.
findings.location: AC #3 / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123
findings.suggested_fix: Following the Contributors pattern (lines 91-107 of setup-project.sh), add: (1) an AUTOSUBMIT_VOTERS_GROUP var, (2) a create-group block with --description and --owner Administrators, (3) a set-members call to add autolander-bot, then (4) the existing want= entry. Reference this creation block in AC #3.

findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `Autosubmit Voters` to `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group-UUID dict (AC #3) but is silent on the preceding `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'` + `gerrit set-members` convergence block that must run before UUID resolution. The existing script (lines 47–107) shows the invariant: every group in `project.config` must have a `create-group`-if-absent + `set-members` block before the `want=` dict attempts to resolve its UUID via `ls-groups`. Without this block, `ls-groups` will not list `Autosubmit Voters`, no UUID row is written to the `groups` file, and Gerrit rejects the `refs/meta/config` push with an 'unknown group' error — breaking the cutover entirely.
findings.location: What section ('Register the group's UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict') and AC #3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a paragraph to the What section (after 'Register the group's UUID…'): 'In `setup-project.sh`, add an `Autosubmit Voters` provisioning block — identical in structure to the `feature-branch-drivers` block (lines 60–74) — that (a) creates the group if absent (`gerrit create-group "Autosubmit Voters" --description "…" --group Administrators`), (b) idempotently adds `autolander-bot` via `gerrit set-members "Autosubmit Voters" --add autolander-bot`, and (c) adds `"Autosubmit Voters"` to the `want=` dict so its UUID is resolved and written to the `groups` file before the `refs/meta/config` push.'

findings.finding: The plan's `setup-project.sh` edit is described only as adding `Autosubmit Voters` to the `want=` UUID dict (line 123), but the script also needs a new `create-group` + `set-members` stanza (parallel to the `feature-branch-drivers` and `Contributors` blocks at lines 60–107) to provision the group before the refs/meta/config push. Without the group-creation stanza, Gerrit will reject the push with 'unknown group', as the script's own comment at lines 51–52 explains.
findings.location: What section: 'Register the group's UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict' / AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a stanza to setup-project.sh section 1b: create `Autosubmit Voters` if absent (`gerrit create-group "Autosubmit Voters" --owner Administrators`), then add `autolander-bot` via `gerrit set-members "Autosubmit Voters" --add autolander-bot` (idempotent). Add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to the `want=` dict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC #3 (and the corresponding `setup-project.sh` implementation) to include a `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'` block and a `gerrit set-members 'Autosubmit Voters' --add autolander-bot` membership-convergence loop, mirroring the existing `feature-branch-drivers` and `Contributors` provisioning pattern — the `want=` dict entry alone is insufficient.
findings.finding: AC #3 specifies only adding `Autosubmit Voters` to `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group-UUID dict, but the existing provisioning pattern (observed in setup-project.sh lines 60–107) requires three distinct steps for any group referenced in project.config: (1) `gerrit create-group` if absent, (2) `gerrit set-members` to converge membership, and (3) UUID resolution via `ls-groups --verbose` into the `want=` dict. Without steps 1–2, if `Autosubmit Voters` does not pre-exist on the server, Gerrit will reject the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error — the same failure mode the existing code's comment explicitly guards against (setup-project.sh line 53–54).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #3 — `Autosubmit Voters` group UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` dict
findings.suggested_fix: Add a provisioning block to setup-project.sh immediately before the `want=` declaration, modelled exactly on lines 60–107: check `gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF 'Autosubmit Voters'`, create if absent with `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters' --description '...' --owner Administrators`, then converge membership with `gerrit set-members 'Autosubmit Voters' --add autolander-bot`, and finally add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to `want=`.

findings.finding: The plan specifies adding 'Autosubmit Voters' to setup-project.sh's `want=` group-UUID dict, but does not specify the corresponding create-if-absent + membership-converge block for the group itself. Without a `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'` + `gerrit set-members` block (analogous to the existing feature-branch-drivers and Contributors blocks), the `want=` entry will fail to resolve a UUID — causing Gerrit to reject the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error — on any run where the group hasn't been pre-created out-of-band.
findings.location: What section (setup-project.sh `want=` dict) / AC#3
findings.suggested_fix:
Insert a group-provisioning block in setup-project.sh (after the Contributors block, before step 2) following the existing pattern:
```bash
AV_GROUP="Autosubmit Voters"
if $GERRIT_SSH gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF -- "$AV_GROUP"; then
  echo "setup-project: group '$AV_GROUP' already exists" >&2
else
  echo "setup-project: creating group '$AV_GROUP'" >&2
  $GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group "$AV_GROUP" \
    --description "Accounts permitted to cast Autosubmit label votes (autolander-bot only)" \
    --owner Administrators
fi
$GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AV_GROUP" --include Administrators >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
for _m in ${AUTOSUBMIT_VOTER_MEMBERS:-autolander-bot}; do
  $GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AV_GROUP" --add "$_m" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
done
```
Then add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to the `want=` dict on line 123.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a prerequisite step (before the cutover AC) that captures `GET /projects/rebar/access > access-before.json` so the post-cutover diff (`diff access-before.json access-after.json`) can be produced as the AC6 evidence artifact.
findings.finding: AC6 requires producing a diff of `GET /projects/rebar/access` vs a pre-cutover snapshot, but no step in the plan describes capturing the pre-cutover snapshot before changes are applied. Without the snapshot the diff cannot be produced, making the security-invariants artifact unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (Security-invariants artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: Insert a new step before the cutover: `curl -u admin:token https://gerrit.example/a/projects/rebar/access > infra/gerrit/snapshots/access-before-autosubmit.json` and reference this file in AC6 as the baseline for the diff.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the `value = -1 Do not land` line unconditional in AC1 (remove 'if the low end is used'), since the label range is already committed to -1..+1 throughout the plan.
findings.finding: The AC wording for the -1 value line is conditional ('if the low end is used'), but the label range is already fixed at -1..+1 in both the ACL line and the label definition. This ambiguity could lead an implementer to omit the `value = -1 Do not land` line while the ACL still allows -1 votes, producing a Gerrit config with a vote value that has no display label.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'value = -1 Do not land if the low end is used'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `value = -1 Do not land (if the low end is used)` with `value = -1 Do not land` as a required (non-conditional) AC1 line, matching the fixed -1..+1 range already specified in the label definition and ACL.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact authenticated curl form for `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` in AC #5 (confirm `/a/` prefix, trailing-slash requirement, and Basic-auth credential source) — or add a one-line probe to `smoke-check.sh` to verify the endpoint responds 200 on this Gerrit instance before committing to it as the canonical post-cutover check.
findings.finding: AC #5 names `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` as the post-cutover verification step, but this REST endpoint has never been exercised in the codebase (zero hits in the repo) and its auth form (/a/ Basic vs unauthenticated) and path sensitivity (the smoke-check.sh found a trailing-slash difference matters on this Gerrit instance) are unspecified — making it Partially-verified for an environment-specific probe.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #5 — `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`
findings.suggested_fix: Use `curl -sS -u "${GERRIT_SSH_USER}:${TOKEN}" "https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit"` (mirroring the authenticated pattern in smoke-check.sh line 42); add a trailing-slash variant as a fallback probe and document which form is canonical for this Gerrit version.

findings.finding: Both REST verification endpoints cited in the ACs omit the required Gerrit authenticated prefix `/a/`: AC#5 writes `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` and AC#6 writes `GET /projects/rebar/access`. Every existing Gerrit REST call in the codebase uses the `/a/` prefix (e.g., `GET /a/projects/rebar/config`, `GET /a/projects/rebar/branches/*`, `GET /a/accounts/self`). On Gerrit 3.14.1, the non-prefixed path returns 401 (or 404) for authenticated resources, so the operator running the post-cutover verification check will get a failure unrelated to the label — silently masking whether the label was actually applied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #5 (`GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`) and AC#6 (`GET /projects/rebar/access` diff)
findings.suggested_fix: AC#5: `verified via GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` (with `-u admin:<token>` or bot credentials). AC#6: `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command to AC2 (or AC6) that verifies `autolander-bot` is a member of `Autosubmit Voters`, e.g. `curl -u admin:token https://gerrit.example/a/groups/Autosubmit%20Voters/members | jq -e '.[] | select(.username=="autolander-bot")'`.
findings.finding: No proving command verifies that `autolander-bot` is actually a member of the `Autosubmit Voters` group. `GET /projects/rebar/access` shows the group has the ACL but does not confirm membership, leaving the key security constraint (bot-only vote) undemonstrated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 and AC6
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'verified by `GET /a/groups/Autosubmit%20Voters/members | jq -e \'.[].username | select(. == "autolander-bot")\'` returning the bot account.'

findings.finding: ADR-0042 does not exist anywhere in the repository. AC6 references it as a pre-existing document to receive a new 'Security invariants section', but `docs/adr/` contains files only up to 0041-* entries and a search for '0042' returns no matches. This false-premise exclusion deletes the work of creating the ADR itself — if the file doesn't exist, the deliverable is authoring a full new ADR, not appending a section.
findings.location: AC6 / 'Security-invariants artifact' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records…' with 'Author `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-security-invariants.md` documenting…' to make clear this is a net-new file, not an amendment.

findings.finding: ADR-0042 is referenced in AC6 ('the ADR-0042 Security invariants section') as an edit/creation target, but this file does not exist anywhere in the repo (docs/adr/ contains ADRs 0001–0041; 0042 is absent). The plan never names ADR-0042 creation as an explicit edit target, even though AC6 requires authoring content within it.
findings.location: AC6: 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records the "non-gating label + bot-only vote ACL" invariant'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add: 'Author `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-security-invariants.md` recording the non-gating label + bot-only vote ACL invariant and its concrete verification checks (the grep and GET diff referenced in AC6).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'author/update the ADR-0042 Security invariants section' to the What and Scope sections so the artifact work is explicitly described and not an orphan AC.
findings.finding: Scope declares 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY' but AC #6 mandates authoring an ADR-0042 Security invariants section artifact (grep evidence, API diff snapshot). This artifact is not described anywhere in the What or Scope sections, making AC #6 an orphan criterion with uncovered work.
findings.location: Scope section vs AC #6
findings.suggested_fix: Extend Scope to: 'Config + ACL + cutover + ADR-0042 Security invariants section update.' Add a bullet to What: 'Update ADR-0042's Security invariants section with the non-gating/bot-only ACL invariant, grep proof, and API diff snapshot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `docs/adr/0042-<slug>.md` as an explicit new-file edit target in the plan's What/Scope section, clarifying that ADR-0042 must be created (not updated) as part of this story's deliverables.
findings.finding: ADR-0042 is named as a concrete artifact in AC #6 ('the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records…') but does not exist anywhere in `docs/adr/` — it is a new file the plan must produce. The plan does not list it as an explicit edit target or file path, making it easy for an executor to treat it as optional documentation rather than a required deliverable.
findings.location: AC #6 / docs/adr/ (directory listing)

findings.finding: The Scope section states 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY' but AC #6 adds a distinct deliverable — authoring an ADR-0042 Security invariants section — that is not listed in Scope, Why, or What. This internal inconsistency could mislead an implementer into omitting the ADR artifact (because Scope says 'ONLY') or into treating it as in-scope without understanding its author/location.
findings.location: Scope section vs. AC #6
findings.suggested_fix: Change Scope to: 'Config + ACL + cutover, plus the ADR-0042 Security invariants entry that records and verifies these controls. The service that reads the label is a separate story.' Also add a note in AC #6 naming the file path (e.g. `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label.md`) so the artifact location is unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the explicit ACL line in the What section with AC #2: decide whether Administrators receive label-Autosubmit vote rights and express that decision as a single consistent ACL block in both the What description and the AC verify step.
findings.finding: The What section's explicit ACL line ('label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters') omits Administrators, while AC #2 specifies the ACL is restricted to 'group Autosubmit Voters ... + Administrators'. An implementer reading What would emit one ACL entry; reading AC #2, two. This is a genuine cross-section contradiction.
findings.location: What section (ACL line) vs AC #2
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, extend the ACL block to match AC #2: add a second line 'label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators' if that is intended, or remove '+ Administrators' from AC #2 if bot-only is the intent.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC2 with the What section: either add an explicit `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` line to the canonical ACL spec, or remove 'Administrators' from AC2 if admin override is not required.
findings.finding: AC2 states the ACL is 'restricted to group Autosubmit Voters + Administrators', but the What section's canonical ACL line only grants `Autosubmit Voters`. It is unspecified whether Administrators should also receive the label-Autosubmit vote right, and if so, whether via a separate ACL line or group membership.
findings.location: What section (ACL line) vs Acceptance Criteria — AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure to AC #5 (or a Rollback section): e.g., 'if GET verification fails, revert via git push origin <pre-cutover-sha>:refs/meta/config and re-run config-check before re-attempting'.
findings.finding: The live cutover to refs/meta/config is an acknowledged, irreversible-in-practice step, but no explicit rollback or back-out plan is provided. AC #5 only says 'live cutover applied … verified via GET'; it does not specify what to do if the push corrupts config or the verification fails.
findings.location: What section ('apply it to live refs/meta/config') and AC #5
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC #5: 'Before pushing, capture the pre-cutover SHA (git rev-parse refs/meta/config); if post-push GET returns non-200 or missing label, immediately execute: git push origin <pre-cutover-sha>:refs/meta/config to restore the prior state.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback procedure to the cutover steps: capture a pre-cutover snapshot of `refs/meta/config` (e.g., `git fetch && git show refs/meta/config > pre-cutover.snapshot`) and document the restore command if the `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` verification fails.
findings.finding: The live `refs/meta/config` cutover write has no specified rollback or recovery procedure if the push fails, is rejected, or produces a corrupt ACL state — and a botched push can affect ALL project ACLs, not just the new label.
findings.location: ## What — 'apply it to live refs/meta/config (operator cutover)'; AC line 5 — 'live cutover applied to refs/meta/config'
findings.suggested_fix: Before the cutover, add: (1) `git fetch origin refs/meta/config && git show FETCH_HEAD > pre-cutover-$(date +%s).snapshot` to capture baseline. (2) After push, if `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` returns non-200 or the access diff is wrong, restore via `git push origin <snapshot-sha>:refs/meta/config`. Document this in the AC or a runbook linked from the story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify at minimum a CI-level or scheduled check (e.g., a `grep` in a config-lint job) that asserts `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters` is present in `refs/meta/config` after every config push, or document the monitoring approach in the Security-invariants artifact.
findings.finding: No observability is specified for ACL drift post-cutover — if the `Autosubmit Voters` ACL is accidentally overwritten by a subsequent `refs/meta/config` push, there is no alert or metric to detect it.
findings.location: AC line 6 — Security-invariants artifact section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RETIRE note to the plan (even one sentence) specifying that label removal requires: (a) deleting the `[label "Autosubmit"]` stanza, (b) removing the ACL line, (c) archiving or deleting the `Autosubmit Voters` group — or explicitly defer retirement to the epic's deprecation story.
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is defined for the `Autosubmit` label or the `Autosubmit Voters` group, leaving shared state that cannot be safely removed if the feature is deprecated or replaced.
findings.location: ## Scope — 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify or add an explicit pointer to where the `autolander-bot` Gerrit service account is provisioned (a `service-user.sh` invocation, a setup script, or a named companion story); if it does not yet exist, add account-creation as a dependency or in-scope deliverable so the ACL is not pushed against an empty group.
findings.finding: The plan treats `autolander-bot` as the concrete, pre-existing Gerrit service account identity ('canonical account/group naming is what the epic and downstream stories reference'), yet zero evidence of this account exists anywhere in the codebase — no provisioning script, no service-user.sh invocation, no gerrit create-account call. The plan's Scope ('Config + ACL + cutover ONLY') implicitly excludes account provisioning, but if the account does not exist when the ACL is pushed, Gerrit will either reject the push or the 'Autosubmit Voters' group will be empty and the ACL will be inert.
findings.location: What section — 'service account **`autolander-bot`**, in group **`Autosubmit Voters`**'; Scope section — 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency clause or an in-scope step: 'Before pushing the ACL, provision the `autolander-bot` Gerrit service account via `infra/gerrit/service-user.sh` (or a new equivalent) and add it as the initial member of `Autosubmit Voters`.' Alternatively, note a named predecessor story that creates this account and gate this story's cutover step on it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the hardcoded `git commit -m` message in `setup-project.sh` (line 148) to include the `Autosubmit` label and `Autosubmit Voters` ACL in its description when making the other edits to that file.
findings.finding: The `setup-project.sh` commit message (line 148) is hardcoded to list the specific labels and ACLs it manages ('LLM-Review + Verified labels + submit requirements + submit-type pin + feature-branch ACLs + Contributors submit gate'). Adding `Autosubmit` label + `Autosubmit Voters` ACL changes the managed state but the plan does not enumerate updating this commit message as an edit target, causing it to become stale documentation embedded in the script.
findings.location: What section / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 148
findings.suggested_fix: Append `+ Autosubmit label + Autosubmit Voters ACL` to the commit message string on line 148.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `exclusiveGroupPermissions = label-Autosubmit` to the `[access "refs/heads/*"]` block (mirroring the `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` and `exclusiveGroupPermissions = pushMerge` defensive pattern already in project.config) so that inherited All-Projects grants on the label cannot bleed through to the `rebar` project.
findings.finding: The `label-Autosubmit` ACL is not marked `exclusiveGroupPermissions = label-Autosubmit`, so a future All-Projects change granting `label-Autosubmit` to a broader group (e.g., Registered Users) would silently bleed through to the `rebar` project, allowing an untrusted actor to cast the 'please land this' intent vote.
findings.location: What section — ACL line: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`; AC #2
findings.suggested_fix:
In `infra/gerrit/project.config`, within `[access "refs/heads/*"]`, add:
```
exclusiveGroupPermissions = label-Autosubmit
```
This follows the exact same pattern as `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` and `exclusiveGroupPermissions = pushMerge` already present in the file, and ensures the bot-only ACL is the sole effective grant even if All-Projects is later modified.

findings.finding: The plan names `autolander-bot` as the concrete service-account identity and states it must be placed in `Autosubmit Voters`, but no provisioning script or script stanza is identified as an edit target to CREATE the `autolander-bot` Gerrit account. The existing pattern (setup-ci-service-account.sh, service-user.sh) is not referenced or extended, leaving account creation an unenumerated required edit.
findings.location: What section: 'Vote ACL restricted to the concrete bot identity: service account `autolander-bot`' / AC1 and AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'Create ADR-0042' (or extend an existing design doc / CLAUDE.md section) as an explicit work item in the 'What' section, and ensure it covers both the security invariants AND the integration contract (label semantics, vote range meaning, autolander-bot identity) for downstream story authors.
findings.finding: The 'What' section and work-item list omit ADR-0042 creation as an explicit deliverable; it appears only in AC #6. Downstream stories and epic docs that reference this plan's canonical account/group naming will have no developer-facing reference document (e.g., CLAUDE.md entry or label-contract doc) explaining how agents discover and set the label — only a security-invariants section inside ADR-0042, which is audit-oriented rather than integration-oriented.
findings.location: ## What / Acceptance Criteria AC #6
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add: 'Create/update ADR-0042 with two sections: (1) Security invariants (non-gating, bot-only ACL proofs as in AC #6); (2) Integration contract (label name, -1/0/+1 semantics, autolander-bot identity, how downstream agents authenticate and set votes).' Reference this ADR from CLAUDE.md under a 'Label Contracts' heading.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan how the `Autosubmit Voters` group UUID is managed across environments (e.g., dynamic lookup via `GET /groups/Autosubmit+Voters` at setup time, or a per-environment override map in setup-project.sh) so the convergence script is portable.
findings.finding: The plan instructs registering the `Autosubmit Voters` group UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` dict but does not address how the UUID is obtained or kept consistent across environments (dev, staging, prod). Gerrit group UUIDs are server-specific; hardcoding a single UUID works only for one environment.
findings.location: ## What — 'Register the group's UUID in setup-project.sh's want= group dict'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm and document why `autolander-bot` requires its own `Autosubmit Voters` group rather than joining the existing `Service Users` group (the pattern used by all current bot labels); if the separation is intentional for security or identity reasons, record that rationale in the plan's Why/What section.
findings.finding: The plan introduces 'Autosubmit Voters' as a new dedicated per-label group (0 existing call-sites for this pattern), while the established codebase pattern for bot-only vote ACLs uses the shared 'Service Users' group for every label (both label-LLM-Review and label-Verified are restricted to 'Service Users', not dedicated per-label groups). The plan does not affirmatively justify why autolander-bot cannot be added to Service Users instead, making the new-group abstraction a Rule-of-Three miss at 0/3.
findings.location: What section: 'Vote ACL restricted to the concrete bot identity: service account autolander-bot, in group Autosubmit Voters'; AC #2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Why or What section explaining the specific reason for a dedicated group over Service Users — e.g., 'autolander-bot must NOT inherit the label-LLM-Review and label-Verified vote rights that Service Users membership would grant, so a dedicated group is used.' If that reasoning doesn't apply, consider adding autolander-bot to Service Users directly (matching the existing pattern) and omitting the new group.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-paragraph 'Alternatives considered' note in the 'Why' section explaining why a custom label was preferred over Gerrit hashtags (`PUT /changes/{id}/hashtags`) and/or change custom attributes as the machine-readable opt-in lease signal — specifically addressing: (a) ACL granularity (hashtags require no separate label ACL), (b) the label's explicit vote-value semantics (+1/-1) vs. a binary hashtag presence, and (c) why the added config complexity is worth it.
findings.finding: The plan chooses a custom Gerrit label as the 'opt-in lease' mechanism but does not compare this choice against established Gerrit alternatives — in particular, hashtags (`PUT /changes/{id}/hashtags`, which carry machine-readable intent with no label definition, no ACL stanza, and auto-drop with the change) and change custom attributes. Both are first-party Gerrit 3.x features purpose-built for machine-readable per-change metadata. The plan commits to a novel pattern (label-as-lease) without ruling out simpler, lower-complexity standard mechanisms.
findings.location: Why / What sections — label design rationale
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Why' section, add: 'Alternatives considered: (1) Gerrit hashtags — rejected because hashtags offer no native -1 "do not land" veto signal and cannot be ACL-gated to a specific bot identity without a plugin; (2) custom attributes — rejected because Gerrit's REST-level custom attributes have no native UI visibility or vote-carry semantics. A label was chosen for its explicit multi-value ACL model (+1 opt-in, -1 veto) and native Gerrit UI rendering.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence in the 'What' section explaining why `Autosubmit Voters` is a new dedicated group rather than reusing `Service Users` — specifically: that restricting the vote to only `autolander-bot` (and not the LLM-review bot or CI bot, which are also `Service Users`) is an intentional least-privilege design that a tighter group enforces.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `Autosubmit Voters` group (containing `autolander-bot`) as the sole vote grantee, breaking the existing pattern where all service bots are added to the `Service Users` group — but provides no justification for why `autolander-bot` should not simply be added to `Service Users` and have the label ACL granted to that existing group.
findings.location: What section — 'Vote ACL restricted to the concrete bot identity: service account `autolander-bot`, in group `Autosubmit Voters`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'A separate `Autosubmit Voters` group (not `Service Users`) is used so that only `autolander-bot` — not the LLM-review or CI service accounts — can cast an Autosubmit vote; this implements least-privilege at the group boundary.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
